name: AI Best Practices for Drupal
summary: Drupal-specific guidance and behavioral evaluation cases for external coding agents.

provenance.note: The supplied Drupal AI discussion identified the suite as a source of Drupal skill evaluation cases.

artifacts.name: Drupal skill evaluation cases
artifacts.question_answered: Does Drupal-specific guidance improve an external coding agent's behavior on bounded Drupal development tasks?
artifacts.mapping_note: The cases are candidates for skill versus no-skill treatments on a locally controlled Drupal substrate.
artifacts.claim_boundary: The upstream cases do not by themselves show successful work against a real Drupal runtime or establish that Drupal improved.

artifacts.name: Inspect AI runner migration
artifacts.question_answered: How can the Drupal skill cases be expressed in a reusable runner rather than a project-specific evaluation loop?
artifacts.mapping_note: Runner convergence can lower adaptation cost but does not map to a lifecycle stage or task family until a concrete task uses it.
artifacts.claim_boundary: Adopting the same runner improves interoperability only; it supplies no agent-performance result and has no scorecard effect.
